name: search_posts
description: Search posts, drafts, and topics by keyword, status, or category — returns matching titles, excerpts, and publication dates sorted by relevance

query.description: Search term: keyword, topic, or phrase to match against post titles and content

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: get_post
description: Get post metadata — title, status, tags, section titles, word counts, dates. Returns metadata only, not content. Use when the user asks about a post's status, structure, or properties

post_id.description: Unique identifier of the post or draft to retrieve

include_history.description: Include version history for the post

name: create_post
description: Create a new draft post with title and optional topic — initializes a post record with empty sections. Does not generate content; use generate_outline or generate_prose to fill sections

topic.description: Topic or theme for the post

name: delete_post
description: Permanently delete a post from the local database — removes all content, metadata, and version history

post_id.description: Unique identifier of the post to delete

name: rollback_post
description: Revert a post to a previous version — restores content and metadata from a specific point in the version history

version.description: Version identifier to revert to (defaults to the immediately previous version)

name: update_section
description: Replace the content of a specific section in a post — the standard write operation after any content generation or revision

post_id.description: Post containing the section to update

name: insert_section
description: Add a new section to a post at a specific position — creates an empty section placeholder with a heading

post_id.description: Post to add the section to

position.description: Insertion position (0-indexed; appends to end if omitted)

name: delete_section
description: Remove a section from a post — deletes the heading and all content within that section

post_id.description: Post containing the section to remove

name: reorder_sections
description: Rearrange the section order within a post — moves sections to new positions without changing their content

order.description: Section titles in the desired order

name: update_metadata
description: Update post-level metadata — title, tags, status, excerpt, or featured image without modifying section content

excerpt.description: Post excerpt or summary for previews and SEO

name: generate_outline
description: Generate outline options for a topic — section headings, key points per section, estimated word counts, and suggested reading order

angle.description: Specific angle, perspective, or constraint to shape the outline

name: generate_prose
description: Write new content from scratch based on a topic and instructions — generates full prose paragraphs from a brief. For developing existing content, use expand_content instead

topic.description: Topic or subject to write about

instructions.description: Specific instructions or brief to guide the writing

tone.description: Target tone or voice (e.g., conversational, technical, formal)

length.description: Target length — short (~200 words), medium (~500), long (~1000)

name: expand_content
description: Develop existing content into fuller prose — takes bullet points, notes, or sparse sections and expands them into complete paragraphs. Requires existing content; use generate_prose for blank sections

source_content.description: Existing content (bullets, notes, sparse text) to expand

instructions.description: Specific guidance on how to expand (emphasis, examples to include)

tone.description: Target tone or voice for the expanded content

name: revise_content
description: Rewrite existing content incorporating feedback or revision instructions — can do heavy restructuring (rework), light editing (polish), or targeted changes (amend)

instructions.description: Revision direction, feedback, or specific changes to incorporate

focus.description: Primary revision focus — what aspect to prioritize

tone.description: Target tone if the revision should shift voice

name: brainstorm_ideas
description: Generate a list of angles, hooks, opening lines, or perspectives for a topic — divergent thinking to explore creative options before committing to a direction

topic.description: Topic or subject to brainstorm around

angle.description: Constraint or direction to shape brainstorming (e.g., 'contrarian takes', 'beginner-friendly')

name: explain_action
description: Explain what Hugo did or plans to do — provides transparency into the writing process, recent actions, or reasoning behind a suggestion

topic.description: What to explain (e.g., 'last edit', 'revision plan', 'outline reasoning')

scope.description: Level of detail in the explanation

name: adjust_tone
description: Shift the tone or voice of content — changes register (formal, casual, technical, conversational), adjusts sentence length and vocabulary complexity without changing meaning

target_tone.description: Desired tone (e.g., 'more conversational', 'more technical', 'formal but warm')

intensity.description: How much to shift — subtle preserves more of the original voice

name: audit_style
description: Compare a post's writing style against the user's published history — checks voice, vocabulary, sentence patterns, formatting conventions, and tonal consistency across the corpus

reference_count.description: Number of previous posts to compare against

name: compare_posts
description: Compare style or structure across two or more specific posts — sentence length, paragraph density, heading patterns, vocabulary overlap, and tonal differences

post_ids.description: List of post IDs to compare (minimum 2)

name: check_grammar
description: Check grammar, spelling, and punctuation — returns a list of issues with line references, severity, and suggested corrections

name: check_readability
description: Compute readability metrics — Flesch-Kincaid grade, average sentence length, passive voice percentage, paragraph density, and vocabulary complexity score

name: detect_issues
description: Find writing quality issues — cliches, filler words, repetitive phrasing, weak transitions, adverb overuse, and hedging language

checks.description: Which issue types to check for

name: analyze_content
description: Analyze content structure and completeness — word count, reading time, section count, missing images, empty sections, missing metadata (tags, excerpt, featured image)

name: lookup_word
description: Look up word definitions, synonyms, antonyms, or usage examples via dictionary and thesaurus — e.g., 'synonym for important', 'definition of ephemeral', 'formal alternatives to good'

word.description: Word or phrase to look up

name: search_reference
description: Search vetted FAQs and curated editorial guidelines — the unstructured equivalent of a style manual. Returns relevant guidance on writing conventions, house style rules, and domain terminology

query.description: Search term or question about writing guidelines

name: analyze_seo
description: Analyze SEO quality of a post — keyword density, meta description presence, title tag optimization, heading structure, internal link count, and image alt text coverage

target_keyword.description: Primary keyword to check density and placement for

name: suggest_keywords
description: Research keywords for a topic — suggests primary and secondary keywords with search volume estimates, competition level, and content angle recommendations

count.description: Number of keyword suggestions to return

name: format_content
description: Format content for publication on a specific platform — adjusts heading levels, image placement, code blocks, pull quotes, and metadata fields for the target platform's conventions

name: normalize_structure
description: Normalize structural formatting across a post — consistent heading hierarchy, list indentation, paragraph spacing, and whitespace cleanup. Does not change wording

name: diff_versions
description: Show side-by-side comparison of two content versions — additions, deletions, and modifications highlighted so the user can evaluate what changed between edits

post_id.description: Post containing the section to diff

section.description: Section title to compare versions of

version_a.description: Version identifier for the left side (e.g., 'previous', 'v2')

version_b.description: Version identifier for the right side (defaults to current)

name: insert_media
description: Add an image or media element to a section — includes URL, alt text for accessibility, and optional caption. Validates the URL and image dimensions before inserting

section.description: Section to insert the media into

url.description: URL of the image or media file

caption.description: Display caption shown below the media

name: check_links
description: Validate all links in a post — checks for broken URLs, missing anchors, redirect chains, and external link health. Returns a report of issues with fix suggestions

name: browse_topics
description: Browse trending topics, saved ideas, and content gaps — filtered by category or source. Returns topic suggestions with relevance scores and content angle ideas

limit.description: Maximum number of ideas to return

name: publish_post
description: Publish or unpublish a post on a platform — makes the post live immediately or removes it from the platform

name: check_platform
description: Check the publication status of a post on a specific platform — returns published URL, publish date, view count, and sync status

name: list_platforms
description: List configured publishing platforms and their connection status — shows API health, credential validity, last sync date, and available features per platform

name: render_preview
description: Render a platform-specific preview of a post — shows exactly how the content will appear when published, including layout, images, and formatting

name: manage_schedule
description: View, reschedule, or cancel a scheduled publication — manages the queue of future-dated posts awaiting automatic publishing

datetime.description: Structured date/time for reschedule action. Use lowercase day names, 24h time, and timezone abbreviation.

name: plan_calendar
description: Generate a content calendar with topics, target dates, and publishing cadence — lays out a schedule over weeks or months for consistent content production

categories.description: Topic categories to include in the calendar

name: plan_series
description: Plan a multi-part blog series — splits a broad theme into installments, defines the narrative arc, assigns subtopics to each part, and sets a suggested publication sequence

part_count.description: Number of installments in the series

name: search_by_time
description: Search posts by date or date range — find posts published, created, or last edited within a specific period

start_date.description: Start of date range (ISO 8601 date, e.g., '2026-01-01')

end_date.description: End of date range (defaults to today if omitted)

date_field.description: Which date field to filter on

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: read_post
description: Display post content for the user to review — use only when the user asks to see, read, or review content. Do NOT use as a precursor to editing; action tools like revise_content and expand_content already load content internally

sections.description: Load only these sections (loads all if omitted)

name: heads_or_tails
description: Preview the first or last N paragraphs of a post — quick glance at content shape and quality without loading the full post into context

end.description: Which end of the post to preview

name: read_outline
description: Display the structural outline of a post for the user to review — section headings, key points, and word counts. Use only when the user asks to see the outline or structure, not as a precursor to editing

name: write_section
description: Write a brand-new section from scratch and insert it into a post — for creating sections that don't exist yet. To rewrite or edit an existing section, use revise_content instead. Post-aware writing, unlike generate_prose which writes standalone text

post_id.description: Post containing the section to write

instructions.description: Guidance on what to write (key points to cover, angle to take)

tone.description: Target tone or voice for the generated content

name: draft_content
description: Generate a quick rough draft — faster and less polished than generate_prose. Gets ideas down on paper before committing to careful writing. Good for first passes and exploration

topic.description: Topic or subject to draft about

instructions.description: Brief guidance on what to cover

length.description: Target length — short (~150 words), medium (~400), long (~800)

name: search_sources
description: Search the web for original sources and citations — academic papers, official reports, authoritative pages that substantiate a claim or provide background for a topic

query.description: Topic, claim, or question to find sources for

limit.description: Maximum number of sources to return

name: search_evidence
description: Search the web for supporting evidence — statistics, data points, expert quotes, and research findings that strengthen an argument or back up a claim

claim.description: The claim or argument to find evidence for

limit.description: Maximum number of evidence items to return

name: search_inspiration
description: Search the web for similar articles and content — see how others approach a topic, what angles are trending, and where content gaps exist. Returns summaries with links

topic.description: Topic to find similar content for

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: coordinate_context
description: Read conversation history and contextual state — provides the skill with relevant prior turns, user intent, and session context

turn_count.description: Number of recent turns to include (when scope=recent)

name: manage_memory
description: Read or write the agent's memory — L1 session scratchpad, L2 persistent user preferences, L3 unvetted business context from documents and domain knowledge

level.description: L1=session scratchpad, L2=persistent preferences, L3=business context

key.description: Memory key to read, write, or delete

name: read_flow_stack
description: Read the current flow stack — slot values, flow metadata, parent flows, and execution state for the active flow chain

scope.description: Which flows to read from the stack

flow_id.description: Specific flow ID to read (when scope=current or parent)

name: handle_ambiguity
description: Call when the user's request is ambiguous and could map to multiple operations. Signal that clarification is needed before proceeding.

clarification.description: The clarifying question to ask the user to disambiguate their request

candidates.description: List of plausible tool names that could apply

name: conversational_response
description: Call when the user's message is conversational, an acknowledgement, or does not require any data operation. Use this to respond naturally without invoking a domain tool.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why a conversational response is appropriate
